Scott Nakama, Esq., LADVA LAW FIRM (415) 296-8844, snakama@ladvalaw.com Attorneys for Plaintiff MARVIN THOMPSON for Plaintiff.
Sandra J. McMullan, Esq., LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C, 415.433.1940, smcmullan@littler.com for Defendants THE DETECTION GROUP, INC., LAURIE CONNOR, and MATT BARTH SUPERIOR

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-07-2022 Continued To Nov-08-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Scott Nakama, Esq., LADVA LAW FIRM, (415) 296-8844, snakama@ladvalaw.com for Plaintiff MARVIN THOMPSON.
Sandra J. McMullan, Esq., LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., 415.433.1940, smcmullan@littler.com, for Defendants THE DETECTION GROUP, INC., LAURIE CONNOR, and MATT BARTH.

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' THE DETECTION GROUP, INC., LAURIE CONNOR and MATT BARTH'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT under CCP 664.6 is denied. (Not. 2:6-9.) Defendants' alternative request for leave to file a cross-complaint against plaintiff to enforce the alleged settlement agreement is granted. (Id. at 2:10-14.)

CCP 664.6 requires "a writing signed by the parties," but defendants point to no writing signed by both sides. However, CCP 664.6 is not the only way to enforce a settlement agreement. (Nicholson v. Barab (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 1671, 1681.) No sanctions are awarded.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Declaration Of Sandra J. Mcmullan In Support Of Defendants The Detection Group, Inc., Laurie Connor, And Matt Barth'S Motion To Enforce Settlement And Request For Sanctions And All Related Documents And Evidence In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-26-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Scott Nakama, Esq., LADVA LAW FIRM, (415) 296-8844, snakama@ladvalaw.com for Plaintiff MARVIN THOMPSON.
Sandra J. McMullan, Esq., LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., 415.433.1940, smcmullan@littler.com, for Defendants THE DETECTION GROUP, INC., LAURIE CONNOR, and MATT BARTH.

Not Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS THE DETECTION GROUP, INC., LAURIE CONNOR and MATT BARTH'S Motion To Seal Declaration Of Sandra J. Mcmullan In Support Of Defendants The Detection Group, Inc., and Laurie Connor's Request For Sanctions.

The motion to seal is granted. Defendants shall prepare an order that states the findings as required by CRC 2.551(d) and that the declaration of McMullan and attached exhibits filed on August 9, 2022 are sealed. The request for sanctions is denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
